projects.title: VTU Results Parser
projects.description: Bulk parsing of image pdfs results..

aboutMe: I like building systems that are difficult to break, fast to use, and predictable. Good software shouldn’t surprise you with random failures at 3 AM. It should just work.

Most of what I care about comes down to performance and security. Software should respect the user’s time and privacy. That means avoiding unnecessary complexity, minimizing bloat, and thinking about threat models before writing code.

I tend to learn by breaking things. My Linux installs have died more times than I can count. Each time I ended up learning something about bootloaders, filesystems, or kernel behavior. I write these things down mostly so I don’t forget them later.

At the end of the day I just like the craft: understanding systems, writing clean code, and solving problems that actually matter. I’m not very interested in shipping features nobody needs or adding complexity for its own sake.
techPhilosophy: Understand the system before abstracting it

papers.title: Process Checkpointing in Linux and the Architectural Barriers in Windows
papers.description: Short investigation comparing how CRIU works internally and why the Windows process model makes this difficult to replicate

downloads.title: Downloads
downloads.description: A central place for app builds, hosted releases, and project-specific download pages.

apps.title: VTU Results Utility
highlights: Process 1 or 100 PDFs at the exact same time.
All parsing happens directly on your computer. No student data is uploaded to the internet.
Automatically calculates totals, percentages, and highlights passed/failed subjects.
Designed to run smoothly even on older campus laptops.
requirements: Prerequisites: Microsoft Excel (to open the generated reports)

apps.title: APStud
apps.summary: Study resource hub focused on quick access to coursework, revision material, and planning tools.
description: APStud is structured as a central workspace for students who need their material organized without wasting time digging through cluttered interfaces.
The downloads page gives it a proper landing spot beside other hosted apps and leaves room for future packaged releases if needed.
highlights: Built for responsive use across devices
